summary: UltraMatrix matrix recording delivers crystal‑clear sound of a top‑tier July ’89 show, with an effortless first set and a soaring Goin' Down the Road > Brokedown Palace sequence.
review: The 7/9/1989 performance is hailed as the second‑best of the legendary July ’89 tour. Reviewers praise a flawless opening set and a second‑set peak that includes Space, an inspired Goin’ Down the Road Feelin’ Bad into Brokedown Palace, and near‑perfect Throwing Stones. The UltraMatrix SBD source is described as the cleanest mix available, offering excellent clarity despite some heat‑induced guitar detuning.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is rated very high (standout first set, strong second‑set peaks) and the recording is praised as excellent; weighted average yields a ~4.6 star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: UltraMatrix SBD described as the cleanest, crisp and balanced mix; only minor guitar detuning noted from hot stadium conditions.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feelin' Bad > Brokedown Palace
show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy opener leads into an effortless first set, while the second set builds with a powerful Space jam and peaks with the Road > Brokedown sequence, keeping the entire stadium engaged.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Excels at dynamics on Bird Song; energizes Goin' Down the Road; delivers an almost perfect Throwing Stones; minor solo stumble on I Know You Rider.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a strong bass presence early in the set (“Check Phil”).
